body_txt: There is an idea ( codified by J. R. R. Tolkien ) that wizards are identified as X the Color. Sometimes, it indicates rank, sometimes jurisdiction, and every now and then the type of magic being used. Oftentimes, magic itself will specifically be color-coded, with different types of magic or specific magical orders or traditions being equated with specific colors. White Magic and Black Magic , of course, are the most common examples, representing good and evil magic respectively. "Green Magic" is another recurring type, typically referring to magic dealing with plants, nature or healing . (The magic of evil spellcasters might often have a Sickly Green Glow , but it is rare for what they do to be called Green Magic; it is unrelated to the more benign nature-wizard form.) Magic dealing with Elemental Powers may also be color-coded in this manner — red for fire magic , brown for earth magic, blue for water magic and so on. This may also overlap with Magical Sensory Effect . In other situations, individual wizards may be personally associated with specific colors for a variety of reasons, such as using one of the aforementioned types of magic, a signature dress style, hair color or any other relevant cause. A Sub-Trope of Color-Coded for Your Convenience and Technicolor Magic . Compare to Paranormal Palette , which may overlap with this if a spellcaster is a Necromancer , warlock, or witch. A Sister Trope to Color-Coded Elements .
